page: Amazon Web Services m6idn.4xlarge is a general-purpose, network and EBS-optimized server instance powered by the AWS Nitro hypervisor. It features an x86_64 Intel Xeon 8375C processor running at 3.5 GHz, configured with 16 dedicated vCPUs across 8 physical cores. The system includes 64.0 GB of DDR4 memory operating at 3 GHz, yielding 4.0 GB of RAM per core. Local storage is provided via a bundled 950 GB NVMe SSD instance store volume, and the network interface supports a baseline bandwidth of 25 Gbps.

Benchmark results place this instance in the average performance tier across multiple workloads. The Intel Xeon processor achieves a stress-ng single-core score of 1890.79 and a multi-core score of 15409.45, supported by hardware-accelerated instructions including AVX-512 and AES cryptography. Memory performance is characterized by a read-only small block cached bandwidth of 829.41 GB/sec, which drops to 97.47 GB/sec for large uncached blocks. In database and web serving tests, the instance handles 16.8 million Redis SET operations per second and delivers a static web serving throughput of 22.26 GB/sec. For lightweight machine learning tasks, the system processes 135M parameter LLM prompts at 911.07 tokens per second, though performance scales down to 32.48 tokens per second for 7B parameter models.

From a resource density perspective, the m6idn.4xlarge offers a balanced hardware profile, combining dedicated compute resources with high-speed local NVMe storage and enhanced networking. This configuration is qualitatively cost-efficient for workloads that require high-speed local scratch space and consistent network throughput without the overhead of external storage. The instance is suited for database operations, web serving, software compilation, and moderate data processing tasks.
description: Amazon Web Services m6idn.4xlarge is a general-purpose, network and EBS-optimized server instance running on the AWS Nitro hypervisor. It features a 16-vCPU Intel Xeon 8375C processor with a 3.5 GHz clock speed, 64.0 GB of DDR4 memory, and a bundled 950 GB local NVMe SSD. The instance delivers a baseline network bandwidth of 25 Gbps. In performance benchmarks, the server demonstrates balanced capabilities, achieving a stress-ng multi-core score of 15409.45 and handling 16.8 million Redis SET operations per second. Its memory subsystem provides up to 829.41 GB/sec cached read bandwidth. Lacking a dedicated GPU, it is designed for non-accelerated workloads. The combination of dedicated compute, high-speed local storage, and robust networking makes this instance well-suited for database hosting, static web serving, software compilation, and general-purpose enterprise applications.
og_description: Amazon Web Services m6idn.4xlarge is a general-purpose instance featuring 16 vCPUs, 64 GB DDR4 RAM, a 950 GB local NVMe SSD, and 25 Gbps baseline network bandwidth on the Nitro hypervisor.

tagline: A balanced x86 server featuring dedicated Intel Xeon compute, high-speed local NVMe storage, and optimized 25 Gbps networking.

64.0 GB of DDR4 memory providing 4.0 GB per core
Bundled 950 GB local NVMe SSD instance store volume
25 Gbps baseline network bandwidth with EBS optimization
Handles 16.8 million Redis SET operations per second and 22.26 GB/sec web serving
Suited for database hosting, software compilation, and general-purpose workloads
